name: Cantabrian Route Natural Trail
name_original: Playa de las Catedrales y Playa das Illas por A Devesa – Mariña Lucense
description: Jogging the easy 2.9-mile (4.6 km) Playa de las Catedrales and Playa das Illas route offers stunning coastal views and unique rock formation
region_intro: Jogging in As Catedrais offers unique opportunities amidst dramatic coastal scenery in Galicia, Spain. The region is characterized by its remarkable natural rock formations, including colossal arches and sea caves, particularly visible along the beach at low tide. Clifftop trails provide panoramic views of the Cantabrian Sea and the unique geological features shaped by oceanic erosion. This landscape provides a varied terrain for runners, from sandy stretches to elevated coastal paths. Best jogging routes in As Catedrais The most popular jogging route is Cantabrian Route Natural Trail, an easy 2.9 miles (4.6 km) trail that takes about 30 minutes to complete. This route explores the stunning rock formations and coastal views from above. Another top favourite among local runners is Praia de Coto loop from Esteiro, a moderate 8.4 miles (13.5 km) path. This route offers a longer coastal experience with varied terrain and scenic outlooks. Local runners also love the Beach of the Cathedrals – Praia das Illas loop from Esteiro, a 7.1 miles (11.4 km) trail leading through coastal paths and offering views of the iconic natural arches, often completed in about 1 hour 10 minutes. Jogging in As Catedrais is defined by dramatic coastal scenery, natural rock formations, and clifftop trails. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy coastal runs to moderate loops. The routes in As Catedrais are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 2,000 runners have used komoot to explore As Catedrais's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in As Catedrais?
A: As Catedrais offers a selection of over 10 dedicated jogging routes, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. These routes allow you to explore the stunning coastal scenery and unique geological formations of the region.

Q: What do other runners appreciate most about the trails in As Catedrais?
A: The running routes in As Catedrais are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 20 reviews. Runners frequently praise the dramatic coastal scenery, the unique rock formations, and the varied terrain that offers both sandy stretches and elevated clifftop paths.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners in As Catedrais?
A: Yes, As Catedrais has several easy running routes perfect for beginners. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991650327/cantabrian-route-natural-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cantabrian Route Natural Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 2.9 miles (4.6 km) long and offers stunning coastal views from above. Another easy choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14986521" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beach of the Cathedrals loop from Esteiro&lt;/a&gt;, covering 3.05 miles (4.9 km).

Q: Can I find longer, more challenging running trails in As Catedrais?
A: For those seeking a longer and more moderate challenge, As Catedrais offers several options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24611177" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Praia de Coto loop from Esteiro&lt;/a&gt; is a popular choice, extending 8.4 miles (13.5 km) with varied terrain and scenic outlooks. Another great moderate route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20497077" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beach of the Cathedrals – Praia das Illas loop from Esteiro&lt;/a&gt;, which is 7.1 miles (11.4 km) and provides views of the iconic natural arches.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in As Catedrais?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in As Catedrais are designed as loops, offering a convenient way to explore the area without retracing your steps. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24611177" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Praia de Coto loop from Esteiro&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20497077" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beach of the Cathedrals – Praia das Illas loop from Esteiro&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31477294" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Praia das Illas – Beach of the Cathedrals loop from Os Castros&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while jogging in As Catedrais?
A: While jogging in As Catedrais, you'll encounter dramatic coastal scenery and unique geological wonders. Key attractions include the colossal rock formations, arches, and sea caves of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/526099" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beach of the Cathedrals&lt;/a&gt; itself, especially visible at low tide. You can also see the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2833444" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Praia das Illas&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3163847" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de los Castros Natural Rock Arch&lt;/a&gt;. The clifftop trails offer panoramic views of the Cantabrian Sea and the ancient quartzite and slate formations.

Q: When is the best time to go running in As Catedrais, considering the tides?
A: The best time to run in As Catedrais depends on your preference. To experience jogging directly on the vast sandy expanse beneath the natural arches of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/526099" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beach of the Cathedrals&lt;/a&gt;, you must visit at low tide. It's crucial to check the tide schedule in advance, as the beach becomes inaccessible and potentially dangerous at high tide. For consistent running surfaces and panoramic views regardless of the tide, the clifftop trails are accessible at any time.

Q: Are there any specific access rules or permits required for running on the beach at As Catedrais?
A: During peak season (typically Easter and summer), access to the &lt;a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/As_Catedrais_beach" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beach of the Cathedrals&lt;/a&gt; often requires a reservation, even for activities like jogging. It's advisable to check the official regional tourism website for current regulations and to book your visit in advance, especially if you plan to run on the beach at low tide.

Q: Is parking available near the running routes in As Catedrais?
A: Yes, there are usually designated parking areas available near the main access points for the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/526099" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beach of the Cathedrals&lt;/a&gt; and the surrounding clifftop trails. These facilities are typically well-signposted, especially during peak tourist seasons.

Q: Are the running trails in As Catedrais suitable for families?
A: Many of the running routes in As Catedrais, particularly the easier coastal paths and clifftop trails, are suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991650327/cantabrian-route-natural-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cantabrian Route Natural Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, is an easy option that offers beautiful views without significant elevation changes, making it enjoyable for families with children who enjoy a good run or walk.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in As Catedrais?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on all trails can vary, generally, dogs are permitted on many of the clifftop trails in As Catedrais, provided they are kept on a leash. However, access to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/526099" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beach of the Cathedrals&lt;/a&gt; itself, especially during peak season and low tide, may have restrictions for pets. It's always best to check local signage or official guidelines before your visit.
tips: Beautiful rock formations at Praia dos Castros.

Las Catedrales beach has been declared a Natural Monument and is considered by many to be one of the most beautiful and unique in all of Spain. To understand what makes it so special, you just have to wait for the tide to go out. At that time, you will be able to walk through it and admire the huge stone arches and caves that have formed this extraordinary construction of nature.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Playa de las Catedrales and Playa das Illas trail?
faqs.answer: Parking is generally available near both Playa de las Catedrales and Praia das Illas. During peak season, especially for Playa de las Catedrales, it's advisable to arrive early as parking can fill up quickly due to regulated access.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or reservation to visit Playa de las Catedrales?
faqs.answer: Yes, during peak season (typically Holy Week and summer months), access to Playa de las Catedrales is regulated to manage visitor numbers and protect the natural environment. You will need to make a reservation in advance. Check the official Galicia tourism website for current requirements and booking information.

faqs.question: Is this jogging route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the clifftop trails are generally accessible for dogs, specific regulations may apply to the beaches themselves, especially during peak season or within the Natural Monument. It's always best to keep your dog on a leash and check local signage for any restrictions, particularly when accessing the sandy areas of Playa de las Catedrales or Praia das Illas.

faqs.question: When is the best time to visit this coastal trail for jogging?
faqs.answer: The best time to visit is during the spring or autumn months when the weather is mild and the crowds are smaller. Summer can be pleasant but expect more visitors. For the full experience of Playa de las Catedrales, you must visit at low tide to walk among the rock formations. Always check the tide tables before planning your visit.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for jogging on this route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily follows comfortable clifftop walking trails and wooden boardwalks, offering a consistent surface suitable for jogging. While the sandy expanse of Playa de las Catedrales at low tide might seem appealing, be aware of uneven sand and rapidly changing tides. The overall difficulty is easy with minimal elevation changes.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary attractions are the stunning geological formations of Playa de las Catedrales, with its impressive 30-meter high natural rock arches and sea caves, and the beautiful Praia das Illas. The clifftop paths offer panoramic views of the coastline and crashing waves, connecting these and other hidden beaches.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner joggers?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is classified as easy and is suitable for most fitness levels, including beginner joggers. The minimal elevation gain and well-maintained paths, including wooden boardwalks, make it a comfortable and enjoyable experience.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails in the area?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other trails. You'll find sections that are part of the Camiño natural do Roteiro do Cantábrico, Mtb Camiño natural do Roteiro do Cantábrico, and Rota da Costa, offering opportunities to extend your exploration of the Mariña Lucense coastline.

faqs.question: Are there cafes or facilities near the trailheads?
faqs.answer: Yes, you'll find cafes, restaurants, and basic facilities in the vicinity of both Playa de las Catedrales and Praia das Illas, especially during tourist season. These can be good spots for refreshments before or after your jog.

faqs.question: What should I wear or bring for jogging on this coastal trail?
faqs.answer: Given it's a coastal route, wear comfortable jogging attire and appropriate footwear for varied surfaces (paths, boardwalks, potentially sand). Bring water, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and a light jacket as coastal weather can change quickly. Don't forget your camera for the stunning views!

faqs.question: What is the best time of day to experience the 'Cathedrals' at Playa de las Catedrales?
faqs.answer: To fully experience the dramatic rock formations and walk among the arches of Playa de las Catedrales, you must visit during low tide. At high tide, the beach is almost entirely submerged. Always consult local tide tables and plan your visit accordingly to ensure safe and full access.
